data.title: Bloomberg: Li Lin moves 20-person trading team to Hong Kong-listed company Bitfire
data.description: Li Lin is transferring his entire trading team of approximately 20 people from his family office, Avenir Group, to Bitfire Group, a Hong Kong-listed wealth management firm. Li Lin reportedly holds a 30% stake in Bitfire, and this move is intended to help the company attract more private banking clients. Bitfire CEO Livio Weng stated that Bitfire will acquire the team and its trading system from Avenir Group. Avenir confirmed the deal. With the addition of the new team, Bitfire has received initial investment intentions of approximately $500 million from family offices and publicly traded companies. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Documentary Claims to Uncover Satoshi Nakamoto's Identity
data.description: The creators of the documentary 'Finding Satoshi' assert that the film has conclusively identified the person behind the pseudonym Satoshi Nakamoto. According to NS3.AI, the filmmakers have chosen not to disclose this information beyond the scope of the project. The documentary also explores Bitcoin's beginnings as a tool for privacy and notes that Satoshi is thought to possess approximately 1.1 million Bitcoin, which have remained untouched.

data.title: Jefferies: KelpDAO security incident may slow down Wall Street's blockchain deployment.
data.description: Wall Street investment bank Jefferies analysis points out that the Kelp DAO attack on April 18, which resulted in approximately $293 million in damage, exposed critical infrastructure risks and may prompt traditional financial institutions to reassess the pace of blockchain and tokenization development. Jefferies believes that the attackers triggered a market sell-off and liquidity shortage by minting uncollateralized tokens and engaging in cross-platform lending. The incident is believed to be potentially linked to Lazarus Group and also highlights the single point of failure in the verification mechanisms of cross-chain bridges. As institutions accelerate the tokenization of assets (such as funds, bonds, and deposits), related risks may cause some banks and asset management institutions to postpone deployment and prioritize system security. Especially in scenarios relying on cross-chain infrastructure, security vulnerabilities could lead to market fragmentation, weakening the actual utility of tokenized assets. Despite the short-term setback in confidence, Jefferies emphasizes that the long-term trend remains unchanged. With regulatory progress and continuous infrastructure improvement, stablecoins and other application scenarios still have growth potential, but the industry as a whole is still in its early stages of development and needs time to improve system robustness. (CoinDesk)

data.title: DDC Enterprise Reports Record Revenue and Launches AI-Driven Platform
data.description: DDC Enterprise, a publicly traded company on the U.S. stock market, has released its fiscal year 2025 earnings report, revealing a record revenue of $39.2 million, marking a 4.6% increase from the previous year. According to Odaily, the company holds approximately 2,383 bitcoins as of April 21, 2026, placing it among the top 30 global publicly listed companies in terms of bitcoin holdings. DDC Enterprise also announced the launch of its AI-driven platform, 'DDC Treasury Intelligence Platform,' designed to optimize bitcoin fund management and capital allocation.

data.title: Blockchain.com launches self-custodied perpetual contract trading, powered by Hyperliquid.
data.description: Blockchain.com announced the launch of perpetual contract trading functionality, powered by Hyperliquid, in its non-custodial DeFi wallet. This allows users to conduct leveraged trading directly within their own wallets without transferring assets to third-party exchanges, achieving full self-custodial control. (PRNewswire)

data.title: A whale sold off 2.9 billion ASTEROID tokens and exchanged them for ETH and BNB worth $1.17 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, one whale sold off 2.9 billion ASTEROID tokens and exchanged them for $1.17 million worth of ETH and BNB, making a profit of $1.16 million. Another whale sold off 3.39 billion ASTEROID tokens and exchanged them for $935,000 worth of ETH, making a profit of $865,000.

data.title: Trump plans to extend measures to ease restrictions on U.S. oil shipments.
data.description: Trump's suspension of the controversial Jones Act, a maritime law that facilitated the transport of oil within the United States during the Iran war, has now been extended—and he wants to maintain it. The 1920 Jones Act, a law that increased shipping costs between U.S. ports, required cargo to be transported by vessels flying the U.S. flag. In response to rising fuel prices due to the Iran war, Trump issued a 60-day waiver of the law on March 18 to streamline oil shipments. According to data provided by the White House, since then, 40 tankers have been able to transport oil between U.S. ports from California to Texas, Florida, and Alaska, increasing the actual available fleet size by 70% and helping to reduce costs. (Jinshi)

data.title: Volo Protocol suffered a $3.5 million loss due to an attack and stated it will cover the user losses itself.
data.description: Volo Protocol issued a security incident update on the X platform, stating that its Vaults were attacked, and approximately $3.5 million in assets (including WBTC, XAUm, and USDC) were transferred out. The team immediately detected the anomaly and notified the Sui Foundation and ecosystem partners, while freezing the relevant vaults to prevent further losses. Currently, all Vaults are suspended pending full post-incident analysis and remediation. The project stated that the vulnerability only affected three specific Vaults; the remaining approximately $28 million in TVL vaults were unaffected, and there was no shared attack path, so the funds are safe. Furthermore, Volo emphasized that it will bear the losses and strive to avoid transferring the risk to users. It is currently collaborating with on-chain security agencies to advance the recovery of funds and the investigation, and will release a full review and remediation plan later.

data.title: KelpDAO hackers have converted most of their ETH into BTC via THORChain cross-chain technology.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Yu Jin, the KelpDAO hackers began laundering and transferring ETH yesterday afternoon, and have so far likely laundered 34,500 ETH (worth $80 million). Most of this ETH was exchanged for BTC through THORChain's cross-chain service, resulting in significant "transit fees" for THORChain: 1. THORChain's trading volume surged to $360 million in the past 24 hours, compared to an average daily trading volume of only $20 million previously. 2. THORChain's platform fee revenue reached $420,000 in the past 24 hours, compared to a daily fee revenue of only $5,000 previously.

data.title: Powell Denies Promises on Interest Rates at Confirmation Hearing
data.description: At his confirmation hearing, Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell refuted claims that U.S. President Donald Trump sought or received any promises regarding interest rates. According to NS3.AI, Powell emphasized the need for fundamental policy reforms at the Federal Reserve, advocating for a new inflation framework, innovative tools, and improved communication methods. Meanwhile, Senator Elizabeth Warren criticized Powell, labeling him as Trump's "puppet" and expressing concerns about his ability to withstand presidential pressure.

data.title: DDC Enterprise Reports Record Revenue and Launches AI-Driven Treasury Platform
data.description: DDC Enterprise has announced its fiscal year 2025 results, revealing a full-year revenue of $39.2 million and approximately 2,383 BTC, valued at around $182 million, on its balance sheet as of April 21, 2026. According to NS3.AI, the company reported a 4.6% year-on-year increase in revenue, marking a record high. In addition, DDC Enterprise has introduced an AI-driven treasury platform designed for Bitcoin fund management and capital allocation.

data.title: Cantor is bullish on Coinbase and Robinhood and has raised its target prices, predicting a new round of market-driven growth.
data.description: Cantor Fitzgerald analysts say investors are shifting their focus to new businesses such as prediction markets, a key driver of the next phase of growth for Coinbase and Robinhood. The analysts maintain an "overweight" rating on both exchanges and have raised their price targets to $250 and $110 respectively, believing that the medium- to long-term growth prospects for both companies improve as product expansion (such as prediction markets, tokenization, and private market access) progresses. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Apple Faces Leadership Transition Amid Talent Retention Challenges
data.description:
Apple's incoming CEO, John Ternus, is set to face a significant challenge as he takes the helm during a period of historic personnel changes. According to Odaily, the company has recently experienced a wave of departures affecting both executives and engineers, following years of relative stability. Ternus, who will succeed Tim Cook as CEO in September, is tasked with stabilizing the workforce.

Late last year, the highly regarded head of Apple's chip business, Johny Srouji, informed Cook of his serious consideration to leave the company. Apple managed to retain Srouji by offering him a new compensation package and an expanded role as the company's first Chief Hardware Officer. Ternus will need to address succession planning and potential departures among other executives, including Mike Rockwell and several long-serving leaders nearing retirement.

data.title: Crypto Market Sentiment Remains Neutral as Fear & Greed Index Rises
data.description: The Fear & Greed Index, a measure of market sentiment, increased by two points to 57, maintaining its position within the neutral range. According to NS3.AI, this index evaluates market sentiment by analyzing the price movements of the top 10 cryptocurrencies, volatility, derivatives data, the Stablecoin Supply Ratio, and platform search data.

data.title: A wallet suspected to be linked to the whale "dimethyltryptamine.eth" purchased 5.66 million SPIKE tokens.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, five hours ago, the Solana on-chain wallet "fent.sol," suspected to be associated with the whale "dimethyltryptamine.eth," bought 5.66 million SPIKE. This whale previously spent 0.125 ETH to buy 5.9 trillion PEPE and sold it at the peak, getting a return of 52,600 times.

data.title: Ether.fi Restores weETH Across All Chains with Enhanced Security Measures
data.description: Ether.fi announced on the X platform that weETH, based on the LayerZero cross-chain bridge, is now restored across all chains. According to Odaily, the platform has also enabled liquidity minting and redemption features. To enhance security, the ether team has increased the number of Decentralized Verification Networks (DVN) from two to four and implemented stricter rate-limiting mechanisms. Additionally, related services will gradually resume under the guidance of security partners, with more updates to follow.

data.title: Apple's new CEO faces challenges in retaining talent: pressure from executive and engineer attrition is rising.
data.description: Apple's next CEO, John Turners, faces a major challenge as he assumes the role: retaining top talent during a period of historic personnel change. After several relatively calm years, Apple recently experienced a wave of departures, affecting both senior executives and junior engineers. Turners, who will succeed Tim Cook as CEO in September, is tasked with stabilizing the workforce. Late last year, the highly acclaimed chip chief, Johnny Sruggie, told Cook he was seriously considering leaving Apple. Apple recently retained Sruggie by offering him a new compensation package and a broader position—the company's first Chief Hardware Officer. Turners will need to address succession issues and the potential departures of other executives, including Mike Rockwell and several long-time leaders nearing retirement. (Jinshi)

data.title: SpaceX announces it has the option to acquire AI programming startup Cursor for $60 billion.
data.description: SpaceX announced on its X platform that it has reached a cooperation agreement with AI programming startup Cursor, which has granted SpaceX the option to acquire it for $60 billion later this year, or pay $10 billion to advance the collaboration. Cursor is a rapidly growing artificial intelligence startup focused on developing code-writing software.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Advocates for Ethereum's Support of Non-Crypto Developers
data.description: Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin has proposed a model for funding non-crypto developers through Ethereum, emphasizing a dependency-based approach. According to NS3.AI, Buterin highlighted the potential for Ethereum to provide financial support, citing an example of 1.5 ETH in assistance. He also urged open-source projects to explore new cryptoeconomic mechanisms to prevent eventual corporate acquisition.

data.title: Spurs vs. Trail Blazers: High Stakes in NBA Playoff Predictions
data.description:
A significant wager has been placed on the NBA playoff matchup between the San Antonio Spurs and the Portland Trail Blazers. According to Odaily, an account with a 46% win rate has invested $800,000 on the Spurs defeating the Trail Blazers, with an opening price of 84.5¢. Additionally, the account has placed a $50,000 bet on the Spurs winning with a spread of 11.5 points, at an opening price of 50.8¢.

The second game of the Western Conference first-round playoff series began at 8 a.m. UTC+8 today, with the Spurs currently leading the series 1-0. The Spurs' starting lineup includes Vassell, Champagnie, Wembanyama, Fox, and Castle, while the Trail Blazers feature Avdija, Kamara, Clingan, Henderson, and Holiday.
